text:
ocosm assumed different shapes and meanings in different
culture-areas.
That man is the principal figure and that the earth on which
he lives is the centre of the universe are wholly in tune with the
crystallized concept of microcosm and macrocosm. An off-
shoot of this human inclination is that the happenings on earth
geographically and in the life of man individually are, of necessity,
influenced by the components of the universe, of stars and
planets. It seems reasonable to suppose that the proto-elements
or earlier forms of astrology could be traced to this inclination
of man. Perhaps, in a!) the ancient civilizations — in Egypt,
Babylonia, India and China — when man the food gatherer
became man the thinker, the nature of the proto-elements of
astrology must have been more or less similar. But those
elements were prone to take different shapes till they reached a
stage of systematization based on recurring experiences over a
long stretch of time.
